item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this. As a gay woman, the anomaly was creepy in a very personal way. The idea that everybody around me could know that I have a 'secret', and become obsessed with discovering it? Even without a compulsive effect, it's chilling. And the sheer oddness of some of the intrusive thoughts listed really helped to amplify that.

That said, I think the ending needs work. I like the concept of a second, opposing anomaly, but I think a debriefing interview log would work better at conveying that information, or maybe even a note from the lead researcher on this SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a second anomaly with the opposite effect.

item.title: Why did I read the bricks
item.content:encoded:
Yay the foundation looks like the good guys in this one

But at what cost AHHHHHH

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it overall, but I'm not sure I buy the idea that while he knows how it happened, it was deemed inconsequential so they just amnesticized him without looking into it? The dude created an anomaly, knows how it was created, but that was inconsequential? I feel like that'd be very valuable information to the foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well this is just simply brilliant. A proper Jamcon entry — almost too short, except for it's just long enough.
